cards.title: Allen AI Releases olmo-eval: An Evaluation Workbench Built for the Model Development Loop
cards.why_it_matters: If you're iterating on model training — adjusting data, architecture, or scale — this is purpose-built tooling for your exact workflow, not just final-model benchmarking.
cards.summary: olmo-eval extends OLMES (Allen AI's open benchmarking standard) into a full development workbench. It supports checkpoint-by-checkpoint evaluation, agentic and multi-turn evals as first-class cases, modular runtime configuration (lightweight direct runs by default, containers only when needed), and statistical tools to distinguish real gains from noise — all designed for the iteration speed of active model development.

cards.title: Hugging Face Jobs Can Now Serve as GitHub Actions Self-Hosted Runners, with GPU Support
cards.why_it_matters: For ML engineers running GPU CI without dedicated infrastructure, this offers a practical path to real CUDA hardware in standard GitHub Actions workflows without maintaining always-on runners.
cards.summary: Hugging Face's jobs-actions bridge routes GitHub Actions jobs to HF Jobs via a dispatcher Space and ephemeral self-hosted runners. Trackio reports ~30% faster CPU CI and a new GPU test suite as a result. The setup uses a GitHub App webhook, a dispatcher HF Space, and short-lived runner registration tokens — GPU flavors like t4-small or a10g-small selectable via runs-on labels.
